title: New EU Battery Regulation published in the Official Journal
bodyHtml:
Regulation on the Management of Batteries from Production to Recycling, aiming to enhance the safety and sustainability of batteries, promote competitiveness in the battery sector within the EU, and cover the entire process from production to reuse and recycling of batteries, including waste batteries, was published in the Official Journal of the European Union on July 28, 2023 (L 191/1). A summary of the regulation is provided below:

The regulation applies to all batteries, including waste portable batteries, electric vehicle batteries, industrial batteries, start, lighting, and ignition (SLI) batteries primarily used in vehicles and machinery, as well as batteries for light vehicles such as electric bicycles and e-scooters.

It covers not only batteries manufactured within the EU but also all batteries placed on the EU market. Therefore, it requires compliance with product regulations through conformity assessment procedures and approved bodies, similar to products placed on the EU market.

The regulation also includes provisions regarding the treatment of batteries at the end of their useful life, aiming to encourage the creation of recycling markets and support the development of markets for secondary raw materials obtained from waste batteries.

Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) is introduced for manufacturers regarding batteries that they introduce to the EU market for the first time.

To enable traceability, a Digital Battery Passport system will be implemented, containing detailed information about battery models, contents, and recycling conditions.

Following specified periods in the regulation, rules are established concerning the maximum content of cobalt, nickel, lead, and lithium that can be obtained from waste batteries.

For accessing the regulation, which is set to come into effect on the 20th day following its publication, please click here.
